cot_prefix: Okay, let's find out which statement about Wu Jinyou is true based on the given passage. First, I need to locate the part of the text that mentions Wu Jinyou. Scanning through the article, I see there's a paragraph that says: "Wu Jinyou, head of the Polar Research Institute of China, and the group's leader, said, "I hope the students have not only a field trip to the Yellow River station, but are also touched by the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers here. I also expect in the future, some of them will choose to devote themselves to polar research and maybe even work with us."" Now let's go through each of the answer choices: (A) He is a graduate from Dalian Marinetime University. The passage doesn't mention anything about Wu Jinyou's educational background or where he graduated from. It only mentions Zheng Li as a student from Dalian Marinetime University. So this is not supported by the text. (B) He is the president of Dalian Marinetime University. Again, this is not mentioned anywhere in the passage. The article only mentions Zheng Li as a student from that university. So this is incorrect. (C) He thinks highly of the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers. Looking at Wu Jinyou's quote, he says: "I hope the students have not only a field trip to the Yellow River station, but are also touched by the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers here." This indicates that he acknowledges and values the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers. So this appears to be true. (D) He is sure that all the ten students will make good polar researchers. In his quote, Wu Jinyou says: "I also expect in the future, some of them will choose to devote themselves to polar research and maybe even work with us." The key word here is "some" -
